scientific_claim: Fiber tissues have higher global CHH methylation than ovules and leaves
source_reported_content: The paper reports CHH methylation levels much higher in fibers (~14%) than ovules (~8.1%) and leaves (~7.8%), while CG and CHG levels were similar among tissues
study_system: Gossypium hirsutum TM-1 leaves, ovules, fibers
limitation: 14 DPA ovule has one replicate in provided text
source_location: Results: DNA methylation dynamics in cotton leaves, ovules, and fibers

scientific_claim: 24-nt siRNAs are higher in cotton ovules and fibers than leaves
source_reported_content: The paper reports small RNA-seq data with three replicates showing 24-nt siRNAs were much higher in fibers and ovules than leaves
study_system: Gossypium hirsutum TM-1 leaves, ovules, fibers
limitation: does not alone prove siRNAs cause all CHH methylation changes
source_location: Results: DNA methylation dynamics in cotton leaves, ovules, and fibers

scientific_claim: OL and FO CHH hypermethylation occupy different genomic compartments
source_reported_content: OL CHH hypermethylation correlated with gene-rich/euchromatic regions and siRNAs; FO CHH hypermethylation was enriched in TE- and repeat-rich heterochromatic regions
data_type: CHH DMRs; siRNA density; gene/TE density
confidence_level: high for distribution; moderate for pathway inference
limitation: genomic correlation does not prove enzyme causality
source_location: Results: DNA methylation dynamics; Differentially methylated regions

scientific_claim: The authors identified many CHH hypermethylated DMRs in ovule-leaf and fiber-ovule comparisons
source_reported_content: 39,668 OL CHH-hyper DMRs and 124,681 FO CHH-hyper DMRs were identified; a subset was validated by bisulfite sequencing of cloned genomic fragments
method: 100-bp sliding-window DMR calling; cloned-fragment bisulfite validation
limitation: validation subset details require manual PDF check
source_location: Results: Differentially methylated regions between the ovule and leaf and the fiber and ovule

scientific_claim: Ovule promoter/upstream CHH hypermethylation is positively associated with ovule-preferred gene expression
source_reported_content: Up-regulated genes in ovule were enriched among genes overlapping OL CHH-hyper DMRs in upstream 1-kb sequences; ovule-preferred gene promoters had higher siRNA levels in ovules than leaves
data_type: gene expression; CHH DMRs; siRNA abundance
limitation: directionality between methylation and expression remains unresolved
source_location: Results: Effects of OL CHH-hyper DMRs on gene expression

scientific_claim: FO CHH hypermethylation is associated with TE-rich heterochromatin and possible TE feedback repression
source_reported_content: FO CHH-hyper DMRs were enriched in TEs and correlated with TE density; more TEs were expressed in fibers than ovules or leaves; authors suggest CHH hypermethylation in fibers may serve as feedback to repress TEs
confidence_level: moderate-high for association; moderate for feedback model
limitation: time order and direct repression are not established
source_location: Results: Function of FO CHH hypermethylation

scientific_claim: GhCMT2 upregulation and HISTONE H1 repression may contribute to fiber heterochromatic CHH hypermethylation
source_reported_content: GhCMT2, GhCMT3 and GhMET1 were upregulated in ovules and fibers; many RdDM genes were upregulated in ovules but not fibers; HISTONE H1 was repressed in fibers but not ovules
limitation: expression data are indirect; no cotton CMT2/H1 functional perturbation reported in provided text
source_location: Results: Function of FO CHH hypermethylation
notes: Mark as inference, not causal proof

scientific_claim: DNA methylation inhibition reduces cotton fiber initiation/elongation phenotypes
source_reported_content: 5-aza-dC treatment in cultured ovules suppressed fiber length and ovule size in initiation and elongation stages; quantitative fiber length analysis reported p<0.01
method: in vitro ovule culture; 5-aza-dC treatment; SEM/phenotyping
causality_level: causal perturbation with low pathway specificity
limitation: authors note possible growth inhibition side effects; inhibitor is not pathway-specific
source_location: Results: Function of FO CHH hypermethylation; Methods: Cotton ovule in vitro culture and 5-aza-dC treatment

scientific_claim: Gene-body CHG/CHH methylation is anti-correlated with A/D homoeolog expression bias in fibers
source_reported_content: The paper identified 539 A/D homoeolog pairs differentially methylated at CHG and CHH sites in gene bodies; methylation levels were significantly anti-correlated with homoeolog expression levels
method: MethylC-seq; RNA-seq; blastp; MCScanX; expression-bias analysis
limitation: correlation does not establish direct causality for homoeolog bias
source_location: Results: Role of DNA methylation in the expression of homoeologous genes
